frameworks.name: dorsal/ventral two-stream visual processing
frameworks.one_line: Where vs what pathways dissociate during saccade-time cognitive suppression.

frameworks.name: dual-task interference
frameworks.one_line: Saccade generation and dorsal-stream cognition compete within one subsystem.

core_question: Which cognitive processes are suppressed during saccadic eye movements, and where in the visual system does the interference occur?
key_assumptions: Object recognition is mediated by the ventral stream
Spatial/orientation judgments engage the dorsal stream
Saccade programming relies on dorsal-stream resources
manipulations: Task type: object/nonobject decision vs. left/right facing judgment
key_findings: Saccade distance did not affect response time for object-vs-nonobject decisions
Response time increased with saccade distance for left/right facing judgments
interpretations: Dorsal-stream (where) processes are suppressed during saccades while ventral-stream (what) processes are not
Cognitive saccadic suppression reflects dual-task interference within the dorsal stream
